message:
feat(output): add legacy decision context fallback parsing and extend new_audit constructor usage

Two related improvements to decision context handling in the audit/output
pipeline:

renderers.rs — Legacy decision context backward compatibility:
- Add parse_legacy_decision_context_object() that deserializes arbitrary
  JSON objects when the typed DecisionContext parse fails, enabling
  graceful rendering of older audit records that predate the structured
  DecisionContext schema
- Add legacy_decision_context_value() and
  legacy_decision_context_scalar_pairs() helpers for extracting
  human-readable key=value summaries from arbitrary JSON fields
- Refactor format_decision_context_summary() and
  format_decision_context_evidence() to try typed parse first, then fall
  back to legacy object parsing, so both old and new audit records render
  correctly
- Add two tests: one for legacy objects with explicit evidence arrays,
  one for plain scalar-only legacy objects

main.rs — Extend DecisionContext::new_audit constructor usage:
- Convert four more call sites (build_approval_decision_context,
  build_event_mutation_decision_context, build_ipc_rpc_decision_context,
  build_plan_audit_context) from verbose struct-literal initialization to
  the new_audit() constructor, continuing the refactor started in
  43a8ba0f; eliminates ~40 lines of boilerplate default field
  assignments per call site

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
